frame_analyses.description: Right needle driver grasping needle at mid-shaft position, Penrose drain visible with marked targets
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Needle positioned for entry, drain appears properly positioned

frame_analyses.technique_notes: Entry point appears close to target mark, good needle angle

frame_analyses.technique_notes: Exit point appears well-positioned relative to second mark

frame_analyses.description: Suture pulled through completely, both marks appear properly traversed
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Good suture positioning, no visible gap in drain

Knot pusher being used to advance tied knot down to tissue
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Appropriate knot pusher technique, knot appears secure

frame_analyses.technique_notes: Clean cut of suture ends, knot appears properly seated

penalties.description: Minor deviation from mark positioning, estimated 1-2mm total

confidence_rationale: Clear visualization of suture placement and knot formation phases. Minor uncertainty in exact measurement of suture deviation due to camera angle during needle passage.
cannot_determine: Exact millimeter measurements of suture deviation - estimated from visual assessment
Precise knot tensile strength - appears secure but cannot definitively assess
technique_summary: Competent expert-level performance with efficient needle loading and suture placement, followed by standard extracorporeal knot tying technique. Overall time excellent for the task complexity.
strengths: Efficient needle loading and initial suture placement
No visible drain trauma or gap formation
improvement_suggestions: Focus on more precise needle entry at marked points to eliminate minor deviation penalties
Consider optimizing extracorporeal knot formation time to achieve expert-level benchmarks throughout
